_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma or equivalent (in ENT)
clinical_indications: Obstructive sialadenitis due to calculi or duct stenosis

preauth.label: Clinical notes (detailing signs, symptoms, examination findings, indications for doing the procedure & advice for admission)
Clinical photograph of the affected part
X-ray occlusal view/ USG for suspected Sialodocholithiasis/Sialography/CT/FNAC report

claim.label: Picture of the Gross specimen of the tissue removed

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes, detailing history/Signs and Symptoms indications for doing the procedure & advise for admission submitted?
Was the Clinical photograph of the affected part submitted?
Was the X-ray occlusal view/ USG for suspected sialodocholithiasis/ Sialography /CT / FNAC report submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details submitted?
Was Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was the Picture of the Gross specimen of the tissue removed submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes or imaging reports not indicative of surgery.
Clinical notes missing details on signs, symptoms, examination, indications, or admission advice.
Clinical photograph of affected part missing or unclear.
X-ray occlusal view/USG for suspected Sialodocholithiasis/Sialography/CT/FNAC report missing or not supportive of indication for pre-authorization.
Histopathology report missing or inconsistent with procedure.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.
contraindications: suspected neoplasia of the submandibular gland where fine needle aspiration cytology or core needle biopsy should be carried out to confirm the diagnosis
